title: 10 best herbal capsules for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for jet lag are natural supplements designed to alleviate the effects of traveling across different time zones. These capsules help by regulating sleep patterns, reducing fatigue, and improving alertness. The best herbal capsules for jet lag include Valeriana officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Melissa officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Avena sativa, Zingiber officinale, Panax ginseng, Withania somnifera, and Camellia sinensis. Without these remedies, jet lag can disrupt daily routines, impact work productivity, and negatively affect personal relationships and overall well-being, leading to stress, anxiety, and decreased quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with jet lag because they promote a restful night's sleep, allowing your body to adjust to the new time zone more effectively. The herbal extract in valerian capsules has a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can be exacerbated by travel fatigue. By promoting deep relaxation and improving sleep quality, valerian capsules can help minimize the discomfort associated with jet lag, such as fatigue, irritability, and insomnia, making it easier to adjust to your new surroundings.
remedy_properties: Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Valerian's sedative and relaxant properties can help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite disruptions from travel.
GABA-Enhancing Activity: Valerian root has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which helps to regulate sleep-wake cycles and promote relaxation.
Anxiolytic Effects: Valerian's anxiolytic properties can help reduce feelings of anxiety and stress related to travel, making it easier to adapt to new environments and get a good night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This terpenoid compound helps regulate the body's circadian rhythms, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep-wake cycle after traveling across time zones.
Valtrate: A sesquiterpene compound, valtrate has sedative properties that promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can be beneficial in alleviating jet lag symptoms like insomnia and fatigue.
Valeranone: This alkaloid compound has a sedative effect and can help reduce anxiety and restlessness associated with jet lag, allowing individuals to adapt to their new environment more easily.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are commonly used in capsules for jet lag due to their high concentration of valerenic acids, which promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Seeds: Valerian seeds are sometimes used in combination with roots or rhizomes to enhance the sleep-promoting effects of valerian extracts and support the body's natural sleep-wake cycle.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots and dry them completely in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of the valeriana officinalis powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill gelatin capsules with the valeriana officinalis mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.
Store the herbal valeriana officinalis capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a calming compound called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms of jet lag. By taking lemon balm capsules before bedtime during travel, individuals can promote a restful night's sleep, even when crossing multiple time zones. Additionally, lemon balm's natural sedative properties help reduce stress and anxiety associated with traveling, making it easier to adjust to the new time zone.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel fatigue, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of jet lag.

GABA-enhancing properties: Lemon balm contains compounds that enhance the activity of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep and relaxation, which can aid in adjusting to a new sleep schedule and reducing symptoms of insomnia associated with jet lag.

Stress-reducing adaptogenic effects: Lemon balm's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stressors, such as time changes, by normalizing cortisol levels and promoting feelings of calmness and relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of anxiety and fatigue often experienced during travel.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with jet lag by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule.
Luteolin: This flavonoid helps alleviate jet lag by regulating the body's internal clock and reducing oxidative stress, which can disrupt sleep patterns.
Nepetalactone: This iridoid compound helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep in a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their essential oil content, which helps to alleviate stress and promote relaxation, making them suitable for jet lag relief.
Flowers: Used for their calming and sedative properties, which can aid in reducing the effects of jet lag and promoting a good night's sleep.
Stems: Not commonly used, but sometimes the stems are used along with other parts of the plant to create a more potent and relaxing extract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated and sun is not intense.
Dry the melissa officinalis leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 1 minute.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with a binding agent like gelatin to create a uniform blend in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the blended mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the potency within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a natural combination of herbs that work in harmony to regulate the body's internal clock. The passionflower, which is the primary ingredient, has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and stress, which are often symptoms of jet lag. Additionally, maypop contains calming agents like valerian root, lemon balm, and chamomile that help to reduce symptoms of insomnia, restlessness, and fatigue commonly experienced during travel across time zones. By promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness, maypop capsules can help the body adapt to new routines and rhythms more easily, minimizing the effects of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Helps to promote relaxation, calmness, and reduce anxiety associated with travel stress and jet lag due to its sedative and anxiolytic effects.
Vitex agnus-castus: Supports the body's natural circadian rhythms and helps regulate sleep patterns by modulating the production of hormones such as melatonin and cortisol.
Melatonin-rich Ashwagandha extract: Enhances the body's ability to adapt to new time zones by promoting a regular sleep-wake cycle, reducing jet lag symptoms like insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help alleviate jet lag symptoms by reducing stress and anxiety caused by disrupted circadian rhythms, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality.
Harmane: Acts as an adenosine receptor agonist, which can help increase sleepiness and reduce stress, making it easier to fall asleep and overcome jet lag.

Isotetrandrine: Exhibits sedative properties by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and GABA, to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, thereby helping to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for jet lag due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that help regulate sleep patterns.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for jet lag due to their sedative properties, which help reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Roots: They are used to make capsules for jet lag due to their ability to reduce anxiety and promote a restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Dry the passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin to create a uniform mixture.
Use a capsule filler to fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them.
Label and store the herbal passiflora incarnata capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of adaptogenic herbs that work to regulate the body's internal clock. The capsules help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. By promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality, maidenhair tree capsules can ease the symptoms of jet lag, allowing travelers to adjust to new time zones more quickly and comfortably. This natural remedy is especially beneficial for those who experience frequent or long-distance travel.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help protect against oxidative stress caused by travel and jet lag, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.

Improves circulation and oxygenation: By enhancing blood flow to the brain and other organs, Ginkgo biloba capsules can help alleviate symptoms of fatigue, dizziness, and disorientation that often accompany jet lag.

Neuroprotective and adaptogenic effects: Ginkgo biloba's neuroprotective compounds can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms, reducing the negative impact of time changes on sleep-wake cycles and cognitive function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help mitigate the effects of jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, which can disrupt the body's natural rhythm.

Terpenoids (specifically Bilobalide and Ginkgolides): These terpenoids have antioxidant properties that can help counteract the negative effects of jet lag, such as fatigue, cognitive impairment, and mood swings, by protecting the brain and nervous system from oxidative damage.

Bilobalane (a derivative of Flavonoids and Terpenoids): This compound has been shown to have a positive effect on the body's natural circadian rhythm by regulating the expression of genes involved in the sleep-wake cycle, which can help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of the herb's medicinal compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Seeds: They are rich in ginkgolic acids, which may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to jet lag.
Barks: They contain unique compounds that may aid in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, both of which can be beneficial in alleviating jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Source high-quality ginkgo biloba extract in powder form with a minimum 24% flavonoid content.
Weigh out 250mg of the ginkgo biloba powder using a digital scale.
Mix the powder with a capsule filling agent such as rice flour to achieve a smooth consistency.
Fill 50-60 empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Store the herbal ginkgo biloba capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue skullcap capsules helps with jet lag because they contain Scutellaria lateriflora, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to promote relaxation and reduce stress. By taking blue skullcap capsules, travelers can alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and restlessness. The herb works by calming the nervous system, allowing the body to adjust to new time zones more easily and reducing the disruption caused by long-distance travel.
remedy_properties: Scopolamine: Blue skullcap contains scopolamine, a natural alkaloid that helps regulate sleep-wake cycles and can reduce symptoms of insomnia and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Baicalin: Baicalin, a flavonoid compound found in blue skullcap, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate the discomfort and fatigue associated with traveling across time zones, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.

Acacetin: Acacetin, another flavonoid present in blue skullcap, has been shown to have sedative and hypnotic effects, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of insomnia, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_constituents: Baicalein: Baicalein, a flavonoid compound, helps with jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can be caused by changes in sleep patterns and environment during travel.
Baicalin: Baicalin, a glycoside of baicalein, helps with jet lag by regulating the body's circadian rhythms and improving sleep quality, which is often disrupted during long-distance travel.
Wogonin: Wogonin, a flavone compound, helps with jet lag by reducing anxiety and stress levels, which can be triggered by changes in environment and sleep patterns during travel.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their flavonoid and phenolic content, which may help regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag.
Roots: They are used because of their adaptogenic properties, which may help the body adapt to new time zones and reduce fatigue.
Flowers: They are used due to their bioactive compounds, which may help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of dried aerial parts of scutellaria laxa by cutting them with scissors at dawn.
Dry the harvested plant material in a drying cabinet at 40 degrees celsius for 12 hours.
Crush 100 grams of dried scutellaria laxa into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered scutellaria laxa with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a spoon and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats capsules helps with jet lag because they contain adaptogenic herbs like Ashwagandha, Rhodiola, and Ginseng that work in harmony to regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms. These herbs help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by time changes, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. By regulating cortisol levels and increasing melatonin production, oats capsules help to realign the body's internal clock with the new local time zone, ensuring a smoother transition and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Herbal oats capsules contain saponins, which have sedative effects, helping to promote relaxation and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Antioxidant properties: Oats are rich in antioxidants, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being.

Digestive soothing properties: The fiber-rich content of oats helps soothe digestive issues often experienced during jet lag, such as constipation or diarrhea, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
remedy_constituents: Avenacosides: These saponins help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce the stress associated with jet lag, promoting a more natural sleep-wake cycle.
Fatty acids: The fatty acids present in Avena sativa, particularly oleic acid and linoleic acid, may help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
Silicic acid: This compound may help to regulate the body's circadian rhythms by influencing the production of melatonin, a hormone that plays a crucial role in regulating sleep-wake cycles.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are the primary part used for jet lag capsules due to their high concentration of avenanthramides, which are believed to help with sleep regulation and reduce jet lag symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help with sleep quality and duration, making them a useful addition to jet lag capsules.
Stems: Avena sativa stems contain bioactive compounds that may help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality, making them another commonly used part in jet lag capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa roots and leaves in the summer months when they are at their peak potency.
Clean and dry the harvested avena sativa roots and leaves thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Use a coffee grinder to grind the dried avena sativa roots and leaves into a fine powder.
Mix the ground avena sativa powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or cellulose in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty capsules with the avena sativa powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a natural sedative that promotes relaxation and reduces stress. When traveling across time zones, our bodies can struggle to adjust to the new sleep-wake cycle, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and other symptoms of jet lag. The calming effects of hops capsules help to regulate the body's internal clock, allowing for a smoother transition and reducing the likelihood of disrupted sleep patterns. As a result, individuals taking hops capsules may experience improved sleep quality and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal hops capsules contain adaptogens that help regulate the body's response to stress, which can aid in adjusting to new time zones and minimizing the effects of jet lag.

GABA-Mimetic Effects: Hops contain compounds that mimic the action of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that promotes relaxation and reduces anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite time zone changes.

Sedative Properties: Herbal hops capsules have sedative properties that can help calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing restlessness, which is common when traveling across multiple time zones.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-acid: Alpha-acid, a bitter compound found in hops, has sedative properties that can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce symptoms of jet lag.

Xanthohumol: Xanthohumol, a prenylated flavonoid, has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce fatigue and improve sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
Humulone: Humulone, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, anxiety, and restlessness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Humulus lupulus flowers are commonly used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly humulone and lupulone, which are believed to have sedative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Humulus lupulus are used because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils compared to other parts of the plant, making them a popular choice for supplements.
Buds: Humulus lupulus buds are often used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the humulus lupulus flowers in a single layer at 50 degrees celsius for 4 hours to preserve their potency.
Grind 50 grams of dried humulus lupulus flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or herb mill.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend for capsule filling.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the humulus lupulus gelatin blend and seal tightly.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months to maintain their quality and effectiveness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and regulate sleep patterns. The calming effects of lavender help to ease the body's natural response to time changes, making it easier to fall asleep at night and stay awake during the day. Additionally, lavender's adaptogenic properties allow it to adjust to an individual's unique needs, providing a gentle and soothing aid for overcoming jet lag.
remedy_properties: Calming effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. This can lead to a more restful night's sleep and a smoother transition to the new local time.

Melatonin regulation: Lavender has been shown to help regulate melatonin levels in the body, which is essential for establishing a regular sleep-wake cycle. By supporting natural melatonin production, lavender capsules can help your body adjust to the new time zone faster.

Sedative properties: Lavender's sedative effects can also help promote relaxation and reduce restlessness, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep during travel or when arriving at a destination.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate jet lag symptoms. By promoting relaxation, linalool can aid in readjusting the body's internal clock.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene is known for its calming effects and ability to regulate sleep patterns. It may help alleviate insomnia and other sleep disorders that often accompany jet lag.

Lavandulol: This terpene has been shown to have antioxidant properties and may help reduce inflammation caused by jet lag. Its soothing effects can also promote a restful night's sleep, essential for recovery from travel-induced fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their calming and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain essential oils that promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and reduce anxiety associated with jet lag.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their adaptogenic properties, which can help the body adjust to changes in time zones and reduce fatigue caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of lavandula angustifolia flowers at the peak of flowering stage.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 days.
Prepare a capsule filling machine and load it with 250 milligrams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers per capsule.
Fill 100 capsules using the capsule filling machine and store them in a cool dry place.
Label and date the capsules according to good manufacturing practices guidelines and regulations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with jet lag because of its natural ability to regulate digestive rhythms and reduce inflammation. When traveling across time zones, our bodies struggle to adjust to new sleep patterns, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. Herbal peppermint capsules contain menthol, which helps synchronize the body's circadian rhythms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. By calming the digestive system and soothing the mind, peppermint capsules can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, allowing travelers to recover faster and feel more refreshed during their journey.
remedy_properties: Digestive soothing properties: Peppermint oil's ability to relax muscles in the digestive tract can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea caused by jet lag, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with jet lag, including headaches, fatigue, and muscle soreness, allowing the body to adapt more quickly to a new environment.

Stress-relieving properties: The calming effects of peppermint oil can help mitigate the stress and anxiety often experienced during travel, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality, which is essential for recovering from jet lag.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with jet lag by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, which can alleviate the fatigue and discomfort associated with traveling across time zones.

Menthone: Menthone exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation caused by jet lag, promoting a smoother transition to a new sleep-wake cycle.

Limonene: Limonene, a monoterpene in mentha, has been shown to have a positive effect on mood and sleep quality, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing the stress and disruption of jet lag.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Flowers: Used for their calming effects, which can aid in relaxation and sleep regulation after traveling across time zones.
Roots: Used for their ability to enhance digestion and reduce nausea, common issues associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers, ideally in the morning for highest potency.
Dry the harvested mentha x piperita in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 5 grams of the dried mentha x piperita into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of the ground mentha x piperita powder with 500 milligrams of a natural filler material, such as cellulose.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the powder-filler mixture, and seal the capsules immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with jet lag because they contain natural ingredients that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, which are often heightened when traveling across different time zones. The herbal blend in these capsules has a calming effect on the body, helping to regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of rest during travel. Additionally, the lime oil present in the capsules has been shown to stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of jet lag-related fatigue, making it an effective natural remedy for overcoming the discomforts of time zone changes.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal lime capsules contain adaptogens like ashwagandha and ginseng, which help the body adjust to new time zones by normalizing cortisol levels and reducing stress response, thus minimizing the effects of jet lag.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidant compounds in herbal lime capsules, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by air travel, which can exacerbate jet lag symptoms like fatigue and irritability.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Herbal lime capsules often contain soothing herbs like peppermint and ginger, which can help alleviate digestive issues commonly experienced during long-haul flights, such as bloating, cramps, and nausea.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds may help reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone and reduce the effects of jet lag.

Terpenoids (including limonene and borneol): Terpenoids have sedative and relaxing properties, which can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality, ultimately alleviating jet lag symptoms.

Phenolic acids (including rosmarinic acid): Phenolic acids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and promote a sense of calmness, making it easier to adapt to a new time zone and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used in supplements because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help with sleep and relaxation.
Buds: They are used in supplements because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help with anxiety and insomnia.
Seeds: They are used in supplements because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help with sleep and relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried tilia x europaea leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 30°c for 24 hours to preserve its properties.
Grind 10 grams of the dried tilia x europaea into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 100 ml of glycerin in a stainless steel bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of natural herbs that work synergistically to regulate the body's internal clock. The adaptogenic properties of hawthorn help to reduce stress and anxiety, common symptoms associated with travel across time zones. Additionally, the cardio-protective compounds in hawthorn have been shown to improve circulation and oxygenation of the brain, promoting a sense of relaxation and calmness. This allows the body to adjust to new time zones more easily, minimizing disruptions to daily routines.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Hawthorn's vasodilating properties can help to relax blood vessels, reducing stress and anxiety associated with jet lag and promoting a sense of calmness.
Cardioprotective effects: Hawthorn's cardio-protective compounds can help to regulate heartbeat and blood pressure, which can be disrupted by jet lag, and promote overall cardiovascular health.

Adaptogenic properties: Hawthorn's adaptogenic compounds can help the body adapt to new time zones and environments, reducing symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and digestive issues often experienced during jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and stress, making them suitable for alleviating symptoms associated with jet lag.

Barks: The barks of the hawthorn tree contain flavonoids and oligomeric procyanidins that help to regulate blood flow and reduce inflammation, which can be beneficial in addressing fatigue and disorientation caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in late summer.
Dry the harvested material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried crataegus monogyna into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 gram of powdered crataegus monogyna with 50 milliliters of boiling water to create a decoction.
Encapsulate the decoction in gelatin capsules with a dosage of 500 milligrams taken thrice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with jet lag because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have adaptogenic properties. When taken regularly, it can help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by time zone changes. The herb also has anti-inflammatory effects, which can alleviate fatigue and discomfort associated with jet lag. Additionally, licorice capsules contain flavonoids, which can help improve sleep quality and duration, making it easier to adjust to a new timezone.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Herbal licorice capsules can help the body adapt to changes in sleep patterns, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, anxiety, and insomnia by promoting hormonal balance and stress relief.

Digestive Soothing Effects: Licorice root has been shown to calm digestive issues like nausea, bloating, and stomach upset often caused by travel-related food sensitivities or altered eating habits, allowing for a smoother transition to new time zones.

Antioxidant Properties: Licorice root's high antioxidant content helps protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag such as headaches, muscle tension, and general malaise, promoting overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and stress.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried licorice root powder measuring precisely one part by weight.
Mix two grams of licorice root powder with 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass container and discard solids instantly.
Capsule the filtered liquid using empty gelatin capsules containing 250 milligrams per serving size.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two weeks promptly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arabica capsules helps with jet lag because they contain carefully selected herbs that promote a natural adjustment to new time zones. The unique blend of botanicals in these capsules works to regulate the body's internal clock, reducing the effects of fatigue and disorientation often experienced during long-distance travel. By stimulating the production of melatonin and other essential hormones, Arabica capsules help restore a sense of balance and energy, making it easier to adjust to new schedules and environments.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Arabica's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, including time zone changes, by reducing cortisol levels and increasing resilience.
Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidant properties of Arabica help protect against oxidative stress caused by air travel, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and headaches.
Melatonin Regulation: Some herbal Arabica capsules contain melatonin-regulating compounds that help regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, allowing for a smoother transition to local time.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Arabica plant contain a high concentration of caffeine and other bioactive compounds, which help regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Arabica have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat insomnia and other sleep disorders, making them a key component in medicinal capsules for jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality coffea arabica beans from a reputable source and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Grind the dried coffee beans into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to achieve an optimal particle size.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground coffee powder with two teaspoons of valerian root powder and half teaspoon of chamomile flower extract in a small bowl to create a blended formula.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the blended formula, ensuring each capsule contains the exact same dose of 1.5 grams and seals them tightly using a capsule filler machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze them for longer storage to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with jet lag because they promote digestive health, which is often disrupted by long-distance travel. The capsules contain a natural herb that stimulates digestion and alleviates bloating, cramps, and other discomforts associated with traveling across time zones. By regulating bowel movements, fennel capsules help the body adjust to new rhythms, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and irritability. As a result, travelers can enjoy a smoother transition into their destination's time zone and feel more refreshed and focused during their stay.
remedy_properties: Digestive aid: Fennel's natural oils can help alleviate digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort that often accompany jet lag, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by air travel, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag.

Gastrointestinal soothing: Fennel's anti-spasmodic properties can help calm an upset stomach and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of nausea, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal discomforts often associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, fennel roots help regulate body rhythms and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a natural sedative that promotes relaxation and helps adjust to new time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems from your garden or a local botanical store.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare plant material in a warm place for at least 24 hours.
Grind dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one teaspoon of ground foeniculum vulgare with 250mg gelatin capsules and fill with distilled water.
Allow the mixture to set for at least 30 minutes before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with jet lag because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to have a regulating effect on the body's circadian rhythms. When traveling across time zones, our natural sleep-wake cycles are disrupted, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and other symptoms of jet lag. Milk thistle capsules may help normalize these rhythms by promoting relaxation and reducing oxidative stress, allowing your body to adjust to its new environment more quickly and easily.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by jet lag, which can disrupt the body's natural rhythms and make it harder to adjust to new time zones.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties that help to protect the liver from damage caused by changes in diet and environment during travel, allowing the body to adapt more easily to new environments.
Sterols: Help to regulate liver function and improve digestion, which can become disrupted due to changes in diet and water intake during travel, making it easier to adjust to a new timezone.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid that helps protect the liver and reduce oxidative stress caused by jet lag-induced disruptions.
Roots: Contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that supports gut health and helps regulate circadian rhythms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried silybum marianum herb from reputable sources ensuring freshness.
Grind the dried herb into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 3 minutes.
Mix 50g of powdered herb with 250ml of cold distilled water in a clean glass bowl for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 30 minutes allowing for efficient extraction of silymarin compounds.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the cooled herbal liquid extract and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with jet lag because they contain hyperforin, a natural compound that regulates melatonin levels in the body. Melatonin is responsible for governing our sleep-wake cycles, and when our bodies are exposed to different time zones, it can be challenging for them to adjust. Hyperforin works by stimulating the production of melatonin, helping to regulate our circadian rhythms and ease the symptoms of jet lag. As a result, taking St John's wort capsules before traveling can help reduce fatigue, improve sleep quality, and make it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: St. John's Wort contains compounds that help regulate stress hormones and promote adaptability to new environments, reducing feelings of disorientation and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort capsules may help mitigate oxidative stress caused by jet travel, which can contribute to symptoms like headaches, insomnia, and muscle soreness.

Serotonin regulation: St. John's Wort is known for its ability to regulate serotonin levels, a neurotransmitter that helps control mood, appetite, and sleep-wake cycles. By supporting healthy serotonin production, St. John's Wort may help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hyperforin, a key compound responsible for regulating sleep patterns and reducing symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: Contain hypericin, a bioflavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties that helps alleviate fatigue and insomnia associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried hypericum perforatum flowers in quantities of 20 grams.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the ground hypericum powder with gelatin capsules according to the ratio 250 milligrams per capsule fill.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the hypericum mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry capsules helps with jet lag because they contain Berberine, a natural compound that regulates circadian rhythms. When taken as directed, barberry capsules can help reset the body's internal clock, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive discomfort. The herbal remedy also promotes relaxation and calms the mind, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. By supporting the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, barberry capsules can help alleviate the disruptions caused by travel across different time zones.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Barberry contains berberine, a compound that has been shown to regulate body rhythms and improve sleep quality by increasing the production of melatonin, the hormone responsible for regulating our circadian clocks.

Vasodilatory properties: Barberry's vasodilatory effects can help relieve symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, headaches, and insomnia by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the body.

Antioxidant activity: Barberry's rich antioxidant content helps protect cells from oxidative stress caused by travel-related stressors like changes in diet, environment, and sleep patterns, which can contribute to feelings of disorientation and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the barberry plant contain a high concentration of berberine, an alkaloid compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help regulate circadian rhythms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the barberry plant are rich in berberine and other bioactive compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive disturbances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried berberis vulagris roots and stems from a trusted source and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Measure out 250mg of the powdered herb per capsule and mix it with 100mg of filler material such as rice flour or potato starch.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herb and filler mixture and seal them tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months before taking 2-3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild rose capsules helps with jet lag because they contain the natural essence of rose petals, which has been traditionally used to alleviate stress and fatigue. The capsules help regulate the body's internal clock, promoting a sense of calmness and reducing the symptoms associated with jet lag such as insomnia, anxiety, and disorientation. By taking these capsules, travelers can minimize the effects of time zone changes and feel refreshed and revitalized upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Wild rose capsules contain adaptogens, such as rose petals, which help the body adapt to changes in environment and rhythm, reducing stress and promoting relaxation.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds found in wild rose capsules, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by jet lag, promoting a smoother transition to new time zones.

Stress-relieving property: Wild rose capsules contain calming compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, helping alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild rose plant are used to make medicinal capsules due to their calming effects on the nervous system, helping to regulate sleep patterns.
Bark: The bark of the wild rose plant is used to create capsules that can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and calmness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers and dry them immediately for 7 days in a clean, well-ventilated area.
Grind dried rosa rugosa flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 1000mg of valerian root powder and 500mg of lemon balm leaf powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 200mg of the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, ready for use when jet lag strikes.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal capsules are circadian rhythm disorders, seasonal affective disorder, and fatigue. Herbal capsules such as those containing valerian root, ashwagandha, and melatonin can help regulate sleep patterns, alleviate symptoms of insomnia, and reduce feelings of exhaustion and stress. Additionally, herbs like St. John's Wort and Ginkgo biloba may help improve mood and cognitive function. These natural remedies can provide a gentle and non-invasive approach to addressing these common health issues.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with jet lag is a blend of melatonin, valerian root, and ashwagandha. Melatonin helps regulate sleep-wake cycles, while valerian root promotes relaxation and reduces stress. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, aids in adjusting to new time zones and reduces fatigue. This combination can be taken as needed, ideally 30 minutes to 1 hour before bedtime, to help alleviate symptoms of jet lag and promote a restful night's sleep. Consult with a healthcare professional before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis capsules helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and regulate the body's internal clock. The sedative properties of Valerian root help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep in a new time zone. Additionally, Valerian root has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel, allowing the body to adapt to its new environment. This helps to minimize the negative effects of jet lag and promote a smoother transition.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Helps reduce symptoms of jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety through its sedative and anxiolytic properties.
Valeranone: Contributes to the reduction of jet lag by regulating sleep patterns and improving sleep quality due to its sedative and hypnotic effects.
Valerenol: Plays a role in alleviating jet lag by reducing stress and promoting relaxation through its anxiolytic and sedative properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their sedative and calming properties, which help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Stems: Employed for their similar properties to roots and rhizomes, as they also contain valerenic acid and other compounds that aid in reducing sleep disturbances.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried valeriana officinalis root and stems in the morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours to maintain quality.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis root and stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5g of the powder with 5g of gelatin and 5ml of purified water in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with jet lag because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood flow to the brain and enhance cognitive function. This can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, confusion, and difficulty sleeping. Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties also protect against oxidative stress caused by travel across different time zones. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory effects may reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule and recover from long-distance travel.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress caused by travel-induced fatigue.
Bilobalide: A triterpene derivative that promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality, helping the body adjust to a new time zone.
Ginkgolides: These sesquiterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing the symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and headaches.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Seeds: Seeds contain ginkgolides, which are believed to aid in reducing inflammation and improving sleep quality, making them useful for jet lag relief.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree are also used, as they contain compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which may help regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees during autumn or spring when they are young and tender.
Dry the leaves in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 1 to 2 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder at a setting of 5.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with a binding agent like gelatin at a ratio of 1:1 and a liquid excipient like water at a 10% concentration.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine with a capacity of 100 capsules per minute.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis capsules helps with jet lag because they contain the herb lemon balm, which is known to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. As a result, it can help the body adjust to new time zones more quickly. The herb's calming properties can also aid in improving sleep quality, which is often disrupted during jet lag. By regulating the body's internal clock, Melissa officinalis capsules can help alleviate the symptoms of jet lag, allowing travelers to feel more refreshed and energized.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with jet lag by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by long-distance travel, promoting relaxation and reducing fatigue.
Nepetalactone: This iridoid lactone has sedative properties that help regulate the body's internal clock, improve sleep quality, and reduce symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and restlessness.
Geranioside: This terpenoid saponin has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as anxiety, stress, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melissa officinalis are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool, which has been shown to have anti-stress and sleep-promoting properties.
Stems: The stems of Melissa officinalis are also used, as they contain a similar profile of essential oils to the leaves, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue.

Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are sometimes used in combination with the leaves and stems to enhance their sleep-promoting and relaxing effects, although they are less commonly used as a standalone ingredient.
remedy_recipe: Dry the melissa officinalis leaves by placing them in a single layer on paper towels for 2-3 weeks.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with 4 parts of a carrier agent such as cornstarch or rice flour.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dry place protected from light for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata capsules helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and reduce stress associated with long-distance travel. The herb, also known as passionflower, contains flavonoids and alkaloids that interact with the brain's GABA receptors, calming the nervous system and inducing a sense of tranquility. This allows individuals to adjust to new time zones more smoothly, reducing fatigue and the negative effects of jet lag. As a result, travelers can enjoy a more restful and refreshed experience.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
Alkaloids (Specifically Harmane and Harmine): These compounds have a sedative effect, reducing symptoms of insomnia and restlessness associated with jet lag.
Glycosides (Specifically Passiflorine): These compounds have a mild sedative effect, helping to regulate sleep patterns and reduce fatigue caused by jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a higher concentration of active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which help in reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to the plant's sedative and sleep-promoting properties.
Fruits: Serving as a source of flavonoids and other compounds that have been traditionally used to aid in sleep and relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall season.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a clean room at a temperature of 25-30 degrees celsius.
Crush 200 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered passiflora incarnata with 100 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of vegetable cellulose powder.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia capsules helps with jet lag because they contain the essential oil of the lavender plant, known for its calming and regulating properties. The capsules can help to promote a restful night's sleep, which is often disrupted by long-distance travel. By reducing stress and anxiety, Lavandula angustifolia capsules can help to readjust the body's internal clock, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. This can lead to a smoother transition and reduced symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing stress, which can contribute to improved sleep quality and adaptation to a new time zone.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to the sedative properties of Lavandula angustifolia, helping to regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia and fatigue.
Limonene: Exhibits a calming effect and can help reduce anxiety, which is often a significant factor in jet lag, allowing the body to adjust to a new sleep schedule more efficiently.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their calming and sedative properties that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: Used due to their relaxing and soothing effects, which can aid in reducing stress and promoting a good night's sleep.
Seeds: Used due to their potential adaptogenic and sleep-promoting properties, which can help regulate the body's internal clock.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the flowers further in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve the potency.
Grind 50g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1g of the powder with 200g of gelatin in a bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes to gel.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the lavender-gelatin mixture using a capsule filler and seal tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa capsules helps with jet lag because they contain the extract of Avena sativa, also known as oat straw. The herb is believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue. By regulating the body's internal clock, Avena sativa can also help reduce stress and anxiety associated with traveling across time zones, promoting a smoother transition to a new environment.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These flavonoids help alleviate jet lag symptoms by regulating the body's circadian rhythms, promoting relaxation, and reducing stress.
Avenanthramides: These unique compounds have antioxidant properties that may help mitigate oxidative stress caused by jet lag, promoting a faster recovery and adaptation to new time zones.
Fatty acids: These nutrients may help regulate the body's internal clock by influencing the production of melatonin, a hormone that controls sleep-wake cycles and is often disrupted during jet lag.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high content of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) and other compounds that help regulate sleep and reduce stress.
Leaves: Used for their ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can be beneficial in managing jet lag symptoms.
Roots: Used due to their content of bioactive compounds that help regulate the body's internal clock and promote a healthy sleep-wake cycle.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1 part of dried avena sativa root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground avena sativa root powder with 2 parts of a carrier powder such as cornstarch.
Use a gelatin capsule filler to fill 100-200 gelatin capsules with the avena sativa mixture.
Seal the filled capsules by applying gentle pressure to the capsule tops with a capsule sealer.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place to preserve their potency for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with jet lag because they contain ginger, a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant that alleviates symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, nausea, and dizziness. The capsules' active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, also help regulate the body's internal clock by modulating circadian rhythms. By promoting better digestion and reducing inflammation, ginger capsules can aid in a smoother transition between time zones, allowing travelers to feel more refreshed and energized upon arrival. This natural remedy supports a healthier adaptation to jet lag.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with jet lag, allowing the body to recover from the disruption in circadian rhythms.
Shogaols: They help reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can aid in reducing fatigue and promoting a smoother transition to a new time zone.

Volatile oils (such as Zingiberene): These oils have a sedative effect, which can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve the quality of sleep, a crucial aspect of recovering from jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the plant are also used to make capsules, as they contain similar compounds to the rhizomes that aid in reducing the effects of jet lag.
Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are used in some jet lag capsules, as they have been found to have a high concentration of active compounds that help regulate sleep patterns.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of fresh zingiber officinale roots in a dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or coffee grinder.
Mix 250g of the powdered zingiber officinale with 50g of gelatin and 25g of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the zingiber officinale-gelatin-glycerin mixture using a capsule filler.
Label and store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng capsules helps with jet lag because it aids in adapting to new time zones. The herbal supplement is believed to regulate the body's internal clock, promoting a sense of balance and well-being. Ginseng has been traditionally used to enhance energy levels and reduce fatigue, common symptoms experienced by travelers crossing multiple time zones. Its adaptogenic properties also help to normalize sleep patterns, ensuring a smoother transition to the new time zone and reducing the effects of jet lag.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help with jet lag by regulating circadian rhythms and reducing the stress associated with time zone changes.
Panaxynol: This sesquiterpenoid lignan helps to mitigate the effects of jet lag by modulating the body's internal clock and improving sleep quality.
Ginsenolic acid: This phenolic compound helps with jet lag by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can disrupt sleep patterns and contribute to jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of ginsenosides, which are believed to help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Leaves: They contain smaller amounts of ginsenosides and other compounds, but are often used as a supplementary ingredient to enhance the formula.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-10 grams of panax ginseng roots with a healthy appearance.
Clean and dry the roots for 30 minutes to 1 hour in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5-10 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered ginseng with 100 milligrams of vegetable cellulose and 50 milligrams of magnesium stearate in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to maintain freshness and potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera capsules helps with jet lag because they support the body's natural circadian rhythms. This herb, also known as ashwagandha, is an adaptogen that helps to regulate the body's response to stress and fatigue. By reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, Withania somnifera capsules can help to alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and fatigue. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can also help to reduce oxidative stress and promote a sense of well-being, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds, particularly Withaferin A, help alleviate jet lag symptoms by regulating the body's circadian rhythms and promoting relaxation, reducing the negative effects of time zone changes.

Steroidal lactones: These compounds, including Withanolides, help mitigate jet lag by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can be triggered by long-distance travel and disrupted sleep patterns.
Glycosides: These compounds, such as Withanosides, help with jet lag by maintaining the body's natural sleep-wake cycle and improving sleep quality, which is often disrupted due to time zone changes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which help regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce fatigue.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other secondary metabolites that contribute to the plant's adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, aiding in stress relief and sleep quality improvement.
Seeds: They are rich in withanolides, which are known to have sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, helping to mitigate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 250 grams of dried withania somnifera root powder and sift through a fine mesh strainer.
Mix the sifted powder with 10 grams of tapioca starch in a bowl to improve flow.
Add a small amount of water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Use a capsule machine to fill 250 empty capsules with the dough mixture.
Dry the filled capsules in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis capsules helps with jet lag because they contain a concentrated extract of green tea, which is rich in antioxidants and L-theanine. L-theanine promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality, helping the body adjust to new time zones. By regulating the body's internal clock, Camellia sinensis capsules can reduce the symptoms of jet lag, including fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. This natural remedy supports the body's natural rhythms, making it easier to adapt to new environments and time zones.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds help alleviate jet lag by regulating the body's internal clock and reducing inflammation caused by travel-induced stress.
Theaflavins: These flavonoid compounds aid in mitigating the effects of jet lag by protecting against oxidative stress and promoting relaxation, which helps the body adapt to new time zones.

L-Theanine: This amino acid constituent helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation, reducing stress levels, and regulating the body's circadian rhythms, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain a high amount of caffeine, which is the primary active ingredient in jet lag supplements to help increase alertness.
Stems: The stems contain a significant amount of catechins, which are antioxidants that help reduce fatigue and promote a sense of well-being.
Buds: The buds contain a high concentration of L-theanine, an amino acid that can help promote relaxation and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 200g of dried camellia sinensis leaves that have been stored in an airtight container for 6 months.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered camellia sinensis with 2 parts of a binding agent such as gum arabic in a ratio of 1:2.
Fill gelatin capsules with a mixture of the camellia sinensis powder and binding agent using a small spoon for 5 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.
